prerequisites: A Svix account with access to the Svix Dashboard.
A Svix API key, created on the API Access page of the Svix Dashboard. This populates the required api_key field.
A start_date value (the earliest timestamp from which to begin reading data), which is a required configuration field.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Svix authenticates API requests using a secret API key, supplied in the connector's required api_key field. The key is a per-environment secret created on the API Access page of the Svix Dashboard; an incorrect, missing, or outdated key causes Svix to return an error. Treat the key like a password and keep it out of version control.

setupSteps.title: Create a Svix API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Svix Dashboard and open the API Access page. Svix API keys are per-environment secret keys; your organization gets a development environment key by default. Create or copy a key here. You can keep multiple keys per environment to support zero-downtime key rotation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the secret API key into the connector's required api_key field. Svix uses this key to authenticate every API request, so store it securely and do not commit it to version control.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest point in time from which the connector should begin reading data. Records dated before this value are not synced.

sources.title: Svix API Keys (creating and using API keys)
